skill.name: lark-base
skill.description: CLI helper skill for operating Lark/Feishu multidimensional tables (Base). Supports table creation, field management, record CRUD, view configuration, history queries, role/form/dashboard management, workflow automation, and data analysis queries. Use this skill to interact with Base via atomic lark-cli commands.
skill.summary: CLI helper for Lark/Feishu Base operations: table/field/record management, views, workflows, forms, dashboards, roles, and data queries via atomic lark-cli commands.
skill.author: larksuite

security_audit.summary: This is a documentation-only skill for the lark-cli Base module. All 4703 static analysis findings are false positives from pattern-matching on markdown documentation files. The detected 'external_commands' are CLI command syntax examples in documentation (e.g., 'lark-cli base +table-list'), not executable code. The 'filesystem' and 'network' findings reference documentation paths, not actual file or network operations. No source code files (.js, .ts, .py, .sh, etc.) exist in this skill - only SKILL.md and reference markdown files. The skill instructs AI agents to use CLI commands for Lark/Feishu Base operations but contains no executable logic itself. Safe to publish.

content.value_statement: Enables AI agents to operate Lark/Feishu multidimensional tables (Base) through atomic CLI commands, supporting table management, field configuration, record CRUD, view setup, workflow automation, forms, dashboards, and data analysis queries.
actual_capabilities: Create, read, update, and delete data tables via atomic CLI commands
Manage table fields including text, number, date, select, link, formula, and lookup types
Perform record CRUD operations with batch support (up to 500 records per batch)
Configure views with filters, sorts, groups, and cards
Query record history by table and record ID
Create and manage workflow automations with triggers and actions
Build and manage forms and questionnaires
Create and configure dashboards with chart blocks
Perform server-side data aggregation and analysis queries
Manage custom roles and permissions with advanced permission mode
Parse Base and Wiki links to extract proper tokens
Support formula and lookup field creation with expression validation
limitations: Documentation-only skill - requires lark-cli binary to be installed and configured
All list operations must be executed serially, not in parallel
Record list limit is 200 per request; pagination required for more data
Batch record writes limited to 500 records per call with 0.5-1s delay between batches
Formula and lookup fields require reading guide documents before creation
Wiki links must be resolved via get_node API before using as base token
System fields, formula fields, and lookup fields are read-only for record writes
Advanced permission mode must be enabled before managing custom roles

use_cases.title: Table and Field Management
use_cases.description: Create new Base tables with custom field schemas, update field configurations, and manage table lifecycle. Supports all field types including formula and lookup fields for computed values.

use_cases.title: Record Operations and Data Analysis
use_cases.description: Perform CRUD operations on records with batch support, query records with view-based filters, and execute server-side aggregation queries for statistics, rankings, and grouped analysis.

use_cases.title: Workflow Automation and Dashboards
use_cases.description: Build automated workflows with triggers (record add/modify/timer) and actions (create record, send message, AI text generation). Create dashboards with chart blocks for data visualization.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Base Table with Fields
prompt_templates.prompt: I need to create a new table called 'Orders' in my Base with fields: Order ID (auto-number), Customer Name (text), Amount (number), Status (single select with options Pending/Processing/Completed), and Order Date (date). Create the table and fields.
prompt_templates.scenario: Setting up a new Base table structure from scratch

prompt_templates.title: Analyze Data with Aggregation Query
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me the total sales amount grouped by status, and find the top 5 customers by total order amount. Use server-side aggregation to compute these statistics.
prompt_templates.scenario: Performing data analysis and aggregation queries

prompt_templates.title: Create Formula Field for Computed Values
prompt_templates.prompt: Add a formula field called 'Profit Margin' that calculates (Revenue - Cost) / Revenue as a percentage. Also add a formula field to flag orders older than 7 days as 'Overdue'.
prompt_templates.scenario: Creating computed fields with formula expressions

prompt_templates.title: Setup Workflow Automation
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a workflow that triggers when a new order is added with amount greater than 1000, and sends a notification message to the sales team channel with the order details.
prompt_templates.scenario: Building automated workflow with conditional triggers

output_examples.input: Create a table 'Projects' with fields: Name (text), Start Date (date), End Date (date), Status (single select), and a formula field 'Duration Days' that calculates the difference between End Date and Start Date.
output_examples.output: Table 'Projects' created successfully with table_id 'tblXXXXXXXX'. Fields created: Name (text), Start Date (date), End Date (date), Status (single select), Duration Days (formula with expression 'DATEDIF([Start Date], [End Date], "D")').

output_examples.input: Query all records from 'Orders' table where Status is 'Completed' and Amount > 500, sorted by Order Date descending, limit 10 records.
output_examples.output: Found 10 records matching criteria: [{OrderID: 'ORD-001', Customer: 'Acme Corp', Amount: 1200, Status: 'Completed', OrderDate: '2024-03-15'}, ...]

best_practices: Always read field structure with +field-list before writing records to understand field types and writeable fields
Use +data-query for aggregation analysis (SUM, COUNT, GROUP BY) instead of pulling all records with +record-list and computing manually
Read formula-field-guide.md or lookup-field-guide.md before creating computed fields to ensure expression syntax is correct
Execute list operations (+table-list, +field-list, +record-list) serially, not in parallel, to avoid rate limiting
For wiki links, always resolve to obj_token via 'wiki spaces get_node' before using as base-token
anti_patterns: Do not use +record-list for aggregation analysis - use +data-query for server-side computation
Do not attempt to write to system fields, formula fields, or lookup fields - they are read-only
Do not guess table/field names from natural language - always fetch actual names via +table-list / +field-list
Do not pass wiki_token directly as base-token - must resolve via get_node API first
Do not create formula/lookup fields without reading the guide documents first

faq.question: How do I create a formula field that references another table?
faq.answer: First read the formula-field-guide.md. For cross-table references, use TableName.FieldName syntax with CurrentValue for iteration. Example: [Orders].[Amount].FILTER(CurrentValue > 100).SUM() sums amounts over 100. Get target table structure first to verify field names.

faq.question: What's the difference between SetRecordTrigger and ChangeRecordTrigger?
faq.answer: SetRecordTrigger only fires on record modifications, while ChangeRecordTrigger fires on both additions and modifications. If unsure, use ChangeRecordTrigger for broader coverage.

faq.question: How do I handle pagination for large record sets?
faq.answer: Use +record-list with --limit 200 (max allowed) and increment the --offset parameter by 200 for each page until fewer than 200 records are returned.

faq.question: Why can't I write to a field with +record-upsert?
faq.answer: System fields (created time, updated time, creator, modifier), formula fields, and lookup fields are read-only. Only storage fields (text, number, date, select, etc.) can be written. Use +field-list to check field types.

faq.question: How do I enable custom role management?
faq.answer: First enable advanced permission mode with +advperm-enable. Then you can create custom roles with +role-create, update with +role-update, and manage permissions via base_rule_map, table_rule_map, dashboard_rule_map, and docx_rule_map.

faq.question: What should I do if I get 'base_token invalid' error?
faq.answer: If using a wiki link, you must first resolve it via 'lark-cli wiki spaces get_node' and use node.obj_token as the base-token. Do not use wiki_token or full URLs directly.
